neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: Iconic UNESCO colonial center with the pink Parroquia church, art galleries, and cobblestone streets
neighborhoods.character: UNESCO iconic, art galleries, colonial gem

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial houses and boutique apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of San Miguel de Allende. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Miguel de Allende

neighborhoods.name: San Antonio / Los Frailes
neighborhoods.description: Residential areas south of center with newer developments and hot springs access
neighborhoods.character: Residential, hot springs nearby, newer developments
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to San Miguel de Allende's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Miguel de Allende

neighborhoods.name: Atotonilco / Dolores Hidalgo Area
neighborhoods.description: Nearby towns with historic churches, ceramic traditions, and significantly lower costs
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Miguel de Allende

neighborhoods.name: La Lejona / Balcones
neighborhoods.description: Hillside neighborhoods with colonial-style homes, rooftop terraces, and Parroquia views
neighborhoods.character: Hillside colonial, rooftop views, established expat
neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Miguel de Allende
